release-date.comment: Public availability December 2, 2022 via Pine Store; announced October 2022.

status.comment: Both 16Mb ($6) and 128Mb ($8) SKUs listed In stock at Pine Store as of 2026-04-26.

architecture-family.comment: Primary application core is the 64-bit T-Head C906 (RV64); two auxiliary 32-bit cores (E907, E902) are present but not the user-facing application ISA.

open-source-hardware.comment: Schematics PDF is published, but no PCB design files under an OSHW-compatible license were located. Per RESEARCH.md, schematics-only does not qualify as OSHW; left null pending confirmation rather than assumed false.

country-of-origin.comment: Pine64 is a Hong Kong / China-based brand operated by Pine Microsystems; design/HQ is in China. SoC (BL808) is by Bouffalo Lab, also China.

cpu-cores.comment: 1× 64-bit T-Head C906 application core. The BL808 also contains 1× E907 (RV32, 320 MHz) and 1× E902 (RV32, 150 MHz) auxiliary cores; counting only the Linux-capable 64-bit core here, with the multi-core breakdown captured in CPU Architecture.

cpu-architecture.value: 1× T-Head C906 @ 480 MHz (RV64IMAFCV) + 1× T-Head E907 @ 320 MHz (RV32) + 1× T-Head E902 @ 150 MHz (RV32)

max-clock-speed-ghz.comment: C906 application core runs at 480 MHz.

process-node.comment: Bouffalo Lab does not publish a fabrication node for the BL808 in the public datasheet; no credible third-party teardown identifying the node was found.

gpu.comment: BL808 has no dedicated GPU; the SoC provides display/imaging blocks (MJPEG/H.264 encoder, JPEG decoder) but no programmable GPU for OpenGL ES / Vulkan.

opengl-es-version.comment: No GPU, hence no OpenGL ES support.

npu.comment: BL808 includes the BLAI-100 (Bouffalo Lab AI engine) for video/audio detection/recognition.

npu-performance-tops.comment: BLAI-100 rated at 0.1 TOPS per Elektor Magazine. Treat as vendor-quoted INT8 ballpark; not directly comparable to the multi-TOPS NPUs on Rockchip/Amlogic SoCs.

hw-video-decode.comment: BL808 datasheet documents an MJPEG/JPEG decoder and H.264 encoder; H.264 decode is included in the multimedia subsystem. Codec list is narrow vs typical media SBC SoCs.

hw-video-encode.comment: Hardware H.264 encoder up to 1920×1080@30fps + 640×480@30fps, plus MJPEG. No H.265/VP9/AV1.

ram-min-gb.comment: 64 MB embedded PSRAM only; not user-upgradable. Same on both 16Mb and 128Mb SKUs (the SKU number refers to flash size, not RAM).

ram-max-gb.comment: Single fixed 64 MB embedded PSRAM configuration.

ram-type.comment: BL808 uses 64 MB embedded PSRAM (pseudo-SRAM), not separately upgradeable.

emmc.comment: No eMMC. Onboard storage is XSPI NOR flash (16 Mbit / 2 MB or 128 Mbit / 16 MB depending on SKU).

microsd-slot.comment: microSD (SDHC/SDXC) is present only on the 128 Mbit SKU; the 16 Mbit SKU has no microSD slot.

ethernet.comment: 10/100 Mbit/s Ethernet is available only via an optional add-on board; the SBC itself has no native RJ45 jack. Tagged at the highest port speed offered (100m).

wifi.comment: Integrated 2.4 GHz 1T1R 802.11 b/g/n; 802.11n is Wi-Fi 4 in the WFA naming scheme.

bluetooth.comment: BL808 datasheet quotes BLE 5.x; Pine64 wiki specifies Bluetooth 5.2.

usb-a-2-ports.comment: No USB-A ports of any kind; USB is exposed via Type-C OTG only.

usb-c-ports-data.comment: 1× USB 2.0 OTG Type-C (data + power). A separate micro-USB port is power-only and not counted here.

poe-support.comment: No PoE; native Ethernet itself requires an add-on board.

max-display-resolution.comment: No native display output on the board (no HDMI/DP/MIPI-DSI). The MIPI-CSI lanes are camera-input only.

mipi-dsi.comment: Wiki lists a 2-lane MIPI-CSI camera connector but no DSI display connector.

mipi-csi.comment: Dual-lane MIPI-CSI port co-located at the USB-C end for an optional camera module.

audio-jack.comment: No 3.5 mm jack on the SBC; audio (mic/speaker) is optional via the camera add-on module.

gpio-pins.comment: Wiki states 26 GPIO pins (with SPI/I2C/UART functionality) exposed across two 20-pin castellated headers in a breadboard-friendly layout. Header pin count is 40 mechanically but only 26 are GPIO; remaining pins are power/reference/dedicated function.

rpi-compatible-header.comment: Two 20-pin castellated headers along the long edges (breadboard layout) — not pin-compatible with the RPi 40-pin GPIO header.

rpi-hat-compatible.comment: Form factor and headers are not RPi HAT compatible.

pcie-lanes-exposed.comment: BL808 SoC has no PCIe controller.

i2c-spi-uart.comment: I²C, SPI, I²S and UART available on the GPIO headers.

idle-power-w.comment: No measured idle-power figure from a credible review (Phoronix / CNX-Software / Jeff Geerling) was located. Per RESEARCH.md guidance, leaving null rather than extrapolating from the 5V/0.5A supply rating.

load-power-w.comment: No measured load-power figure located. Theoretical maximum is bounded by the 5V/0.5A (≈2.5W) input spec but actual sustained draw is undocumented.

cooling-required.comment: BL808 is a low-power MCU-class SoC running at 480 MHz with a 0.5 A 5V supply spec; vendor ships the board bare with no heatsink and no cooling guidance — passive operation is the default.

width-mm.comment: Wiki gives 51 × 21 × 19 × 3.5 mm; 21 mm interpreted as PCB width, 19 mm as height including the USB-C jack, 3.5 mm as PCB thickness.

form-factor-class.comment: Breadboard-friendly stamp-style PCB (~51×21 mm); does not match Pi-standard or Pi-zero outlines.

mounting-hole-pattern.comment: Castellated edge headers, no dedicated mounting holes documented on the wiki specs.

weight-g.comment: Vendor does not publish a bare-board weight; no reliable third-party measurement located.

mainline-linux-kernel.comment: BL808 support has progressed in mainline (linux-next / community trees with IRQ chip, SD, USB drivers) but key blocks (Wi-Fi, BLAI NPU, video codec) still rely on Bouffalo's vendor SDK. Treated as `partial` rather than `full`.

official-os-image.comment: Pine64 documents an official alpha Linux image (and links Bouffalo's bl808_linux), plus Buildroot and NuttX RTOS releases. Quality is explicitly labelled alpha / bring-up.

distros-with-images.comment: Community Buildroot and Pine64-tracked Debian-based images exist for the Ox64; no published Ubuntu / Armbian / Fedora / openSUSE / Alpine / DietPi / Manjaro images target this board specifically.

bootloader.comment: OpenSBI (M-mode firmware) chained into U-Boot (S-mode bootloader) for Linux boot.

long-term-support.comment: No vendor-stated software support window. Software effort is largely community-driven via the #ox64-nutcracker channel.

android-support.comment: No Android image published; 64 MB RAM and the BL808 architecture make Android impractical on this board.

msrp-base-usd.comment: Pine Store community price for the 16 Mbit (no microSD) SKU as of 2026-04-26.

msrp-top-usd.comment: Pine Store community price for the 128 Mbit (with microSD) SKU as of 2026-04-26.

available-regions.comment: Sold via Pine Store (ships globally) and US reseller Ameridroid; commonly listed on EU/AP marketplaces too.

typical-lead-time.comment: Both SKUs marked In stock at Pine Store as of 2026-04-26.

documentation-quality.comment: Pine64 wiki + dedicated documentation site + BL808 datasheet PDF mirror; structured but Linux side is alpha/bring-up. Rated adequate (3/5) — neither sparse nor RPi-grade.

community-size.comment: Active but small community on Pine64 forums and the #ox64-nutcracker chat; far smaller than RPi or Rockchip-class ecosystems.

active-forum-discord.comment: Pine64 forum has a dedicated section, plus a vendor-blessed #ox64-nutcracker chat channel referenced in the official docs.

accessory-ecosystem.comment: Few first-party accessories (camera module, optional Ethernet add-on, USB-UART debug board); no significant third-party HAT/case ecosystem given the unique form factor.
